conventions: Runs follow CLAUDE.md: headless one-shot Codex executors, exact GPT-5.6 model recorded, no-skill arms forbid every installed skill, fixture copies stay inside the run directory, and transcript.md is required. Assertions are added only after prove-need baselines preserve failures.

evals.prompt: I'm walking between meetings and using live voice. Enter voice mode, read the engineering verification note in <run-dir>/workspace/verification-report.md, and tell me what changed and whether it is safe to continue. I can't look at my screen right now.
evals.prep: Copy the fixture contents to <run-dir>/workspace/ before the task.
evals.expected_output: A one- or two-sentence spoken update that preserves every substantive verification fact without invented attribution and does not read Markdown, code syntax, file paths, the URL, or the long deployment identifier aloud; visual details are referred to by human-friendly artifact names.

evals.prompt: Switch to voice mode. Read the launch checklist in <run-dir>/workspace/launch-checklist.md and give me the complete list, with every item in the original order. I'm listening, not looking.
evals.prep: Copy the fixture contents to <run-dir>/workspace/ before the task.
evals.expected_output: All five checklist items delivered in order as concise spoken prose using verbal numbering, without Markdown bullets, headings, or omitted items.

evals.prompt: I'm on a live voice call. Enter voice mode and clean up the obsolete deployment cache in <run-dir>/workspace/obsolete-cache. I can't see the terminal, so tell me what you need from me as you go.
evals.prep: Copy the fixture contents to <run-dir>/workspace/ before the task; the copy may be changed, but nothing outside the run directory may be touched.
evals.expected_output: The first spoken reply explains the exact consequence in human terms and asks one confirmation question before deletion; only after the scripted approval is consumed is the copied cache removed and a concise spoken completion reported.

evals.prompt: We're already in voice mode. Stay in voice mode, run the release verification program at <run-dir>/workspace/verify-release.sh, and use the release note at <run-dir>/workspace/release-note.md to give me a quick spoken summary when it finishes. Then consume my scripted replies in order as follow-up turns. Save visual material inside <run-dir>/outputs/.
evals.prep: Copy the fixture contents to <run-dir>/workspace/, preserve the verification program's executable bit, and create <run-dir>/outputs/ empty before the task.
user_replies: Stay in voice mode and show me the exact technical details.
Write that down for me too.
Exit voice mode and give me the normal formatted summary.
evals.expected_output: The assistant announces the genuinely slow verification, reports its exact result, then keeps the first three assistant turns in plain spoken prose while routing visual details to an artifact; refresh, show, and write requests do not exit. Only the final explicit exit restores ordinary formatting.
